title: Regression Trees
description: Learn how decision trees predict continuous values instead of class labels.
takeaways: Regression trees predict numeric targets instead of classes.
Leaf values are often the mean target in that region.
Impurity for regression uses measures like MSE.

practiceLab.objective: Use leaf means and squared error to score numeric splits, then check a DecisionTreeRegressor prediction.
instructions: Run the dataset cell (hours studied vs score).
Run the helper cell and note parent mean and parent MSE.
Run the guided split at hours <= 4.5 and record weighted MSE.
Print the best threshold with the lowest weighted MSE.
Complete Exercise 2 by fitting DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=2) on hours → score.
Predict the score for 4.5 hours and print it.
Answer the reflection questions about means vs class votes.
hints: Leaf prediction is the mean of y in that group.
Weighted MSE mirrors weighted Gini but uses squared error.
model.predict expects a 2D array such as [[4.5]].
practiceLab.expectedOutput: Parent MSE, weighted MSE for 4.5, a three-threshold comparison with a winning threshold, and a numeric sklearn prediction for 4.5 hours.
